tex:
(a) Rowena buys and sells clothes.

(i) (b) She buys a jacket for \$40 and sells it for \$45.40.
Calculate the percentage profit.

(c) She sells a dress for \$42.60 after making a profit of 20\% on the cost price.
Calculate the cost price.

\$ 

(d) Sara invests \$500 for 15 years at a rate of 2\% per year simple interest.
Calculate the total interest Sara receives.

\$ 

(e) Tomas has two cars.

(i) (f) The value, today, of one car is \$21 000.
The value of this car decreases exponentially by 18\% each year.
Calculate the value of this car after 5 years.
Give your answer correct to the nearest hundred dollars.

\$ 

(g) The value, today, of the other car is \$15 000.
The value of this car increases exponentially by x \% each year.
After 12 years the value of the car will be \$42 190.
Calculate the value of x.

x =

tex:
(a) The scatter diagram shows the physics mark and the chemistry mark for each of 12 students.

(i) (b) What type of correlation is shown in the scatter diagram?

(c) On the scatter diagram, draw a line of best fit.

(d) Find an estimate of the chemistry mark for another student who has a physics mark of 4.

(e) A teacher records the number of days each of the 24 students in her class are absent.
The frequency table shows the results.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Number of days & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 \\
\hline
Frequency & 10 & 8 & 3 & 2 & 0 & 1 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Find the mode, the median and the mean.

Mode = 
Median = 
Mean = 

(f) Three sizes of eggs are sold in a shop.
The table shows the number of eggs of each size sold in one day.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Size & Small & Medium & Large \\
\hline
Mass ($m$ grams) & $46 < m \leq 52$ & $52 < m \leq 62$ & $62 < m \leq 80$ \\
\hline
Number of eggs sold & 78 & 180 & 162 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(i) (g) Calculate an estimate of the mean mass.

 g

(h) On the grid, draw a histogram to show the information in the table.

tex:
(a) The diagram shows two sets of cards.
Set A: 1, 1, 2, 2, 2
Set B: 0, 1, 1, 1, 2

(i) (b) Jojo chooses two cards at random from Set A without replacement.
Find the probability that the two cards have the same number.

(c) Jojo replaces the two cards.
Kylie then chooses one card at random from Set A and one card at random from Set B.
Find the probability that the two cards have the same number.

(d) Who is the most likely to choose two cards that have the same number?
Show all your working.

(e) Set C: 4, 4, 5, 5, 5
Lena chooses three cards at random from Set C without replacement.
Find the probability that the third card chosen is numbered 4.

(a) At a football match, the price of an adult ticket is \$x and the price of a child ticket is \$(x - 2.50).
There are 18 500 adults and 2400 children attending the football match.
The total amount paid for the tickets is \$320 040.
Find the price of an adult ticket.

\$ 

(b) Factorise $y^{2} + 5y - 84$.

(c) The area of the rectangle is 84 cm$^{2}$.

Find the perimeter.

 cm

(d) In a shop, the price of a monthly magazine is \$m and the price of a weekly magazine is \$(m - 0.75).
One day, the shop receives
\begin{itemize}

(e) \$168 from selling monthly magazines

(f) \$207 from selling weekly magazines.
\end{itemize}
The total number of these magazines sold during this day is 100.

(i) (g) Show that $50m^{2} - 225m + 63 = 0$.

(h) Find the price of a monthly magazine.
Show all your working.

\$

(a) In the pentagon ABCDE, $\angle ACB = \angle AED = 90^{\circ}$.
Triangle ACD is equilateral with side length 12 cm.
DE = BC = 6 cm.

(i) (b) Calculate $\angle BAE$.

$\angle BAE = $ $^{\circ}$

(c) Calculate AB.

AB =  cm

(d) Calculate AE.

AE =  cm

(e) Calculate the area of the pentagon.

 cm$^{2}$

(f) The diagram shows a cuboid.
AB = 8 cm, BC = 4 cm and CR = 5 cm.

(i) (g) Write down the number of planes of symmetry of this cuboid.

(h) Calculate the angle between the diagonal AR and the plane BCRQ.

$^{\circ}$

(a) Water flows through a cylindrical pipe at a speed of 8 cm/s.
The radius of the circular cross-section is 1.5 cm and the pipe is always completely full of water.
Calculate the amount of water that flows through the pipe in 1 hour.
Give your answer in litres.

 litres

(b) The diagram shows three solids.
The base radius of the cone is 6 cm and the slant height is 12 cm.
The radius of the sphere is x cm and the radius of the hemisphere is y cm.
The total surface area of each solid is the same.

(i) (c) Show that the total surface area of the cone is $108\pi r$ cm$^{2}$.
[The curved surface area, A, of a cone with radius r and slant height l is A = $\pi$rl.]

(d) Find the value of x and the value of y.
[The surface area, A, of a sphere with radius r is A = $4\pi r^{2}$.]

x =  cm
y =  cm
